sanskrit:
नैव किञ्चित् करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित्।
पश्यञ् शृण्वन् स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन् गच्छन् स्वपन् श्वसन्॥
प्रलपन् विसृजन् गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन् निमिषन्नपि।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन्॥
transliteration: naiva kiñcit karomīti yukto manyeta tattvavit | paśyañ śṛṇvan spṛśañjighrannaśnan gacchan svapan śvasan || pralapan visṛjan gṛhṇannunmiṣan nimiṣannapi | indriyāṇīndriyārtheṣu vartanta iti dhārayan ||
literal_meaning: The knower of Truth, integrated, thinks 'I do nothing at all,' while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, breathing, talking, releasing, grasping, opening or closing the eyes—holding that only the senses move among sense-objects.
contextual_meaning: True agency is not erased but relocated: the Self is experienced as a witnessing awareness that supervises bodily actions without being tainted by them, dissolving performance anxiety and moral fatigue.
psychological_state: Observing-ego state: calm meta-awareness that decouples self-worth from output, reducing burnout and moral rumination.
trigger_scenario: A caregiver on endless Zoom calls notices tension in shoulders, silently notes 'body is typing, mouth is speaking,' and feels the knot loosen without quitting the task.
anchor_text: Watch life happen through you, not by you.
